title: LlamaCppRuntime KV cache control (quantization q4/q8, occupancy stats)
goal: Implement KvCacheOps for LlamaCppRuntime: set_quantization to q4/q8 via llama-cpp-2 type_k/type_v context params (rebuild context on switch), occupancy stats (bytes_used from llama_cpp_2 kv_cache state, bytes_capacity from n_ctx * n_kv_layers * head_dim * type_size), evict_all (llama_kv_cache_clear).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/kv_cache_impl.rs implementing KvCacheOps for LlamaCppKvCache; integration with context.rs to expose per-model handle; tests.
unblock_conditions: MT-063 KvCacheOps trait + MT-074 generate path merged.
implementation_notes: Per AC-LLAMACPP-ADAPTER + spec §4.7.1b (KV caching PRODUCTION): llama.cpp KV cache quantization shipped 2024 with `--cache-type-k q4_0|q8_0|f16|...` and `--cache-type-v q4_0|q8_0|f16|...`; llama-cpp-2 exposes via LlamaContextParams type_k / type_v. Set quantization path: switching quant level requires context rebuild (cannot mutate existing KV in-place to a new quant); set_quantization records the new policy and triggers a deferred rebuild on next load (or immediate rebuild + invalidate prefix cache if model loaded). Occupancy stats: query llama_cpp_2 LlamaContext::kv_cache_size() or compute manually as `n_ctx * n_layer * n_kv_head * head_dim * (type_size_k + type_size_v)`. Prefix cache: llama.cpp does not have a built-in prefix-share API; implement an in-process LRU layered over llama_cpp_2 by caching `(content_hash, llama_kv_cache_state_snapshot)` tuples. prefix_commit copies the current KV state via llama_state_seq_get_data into a buffer, hashes input tokens, stores LRU entry. prefix_restore uses llama_state_seq_set_data to load the buffer. Replay-resistant: on restore, recompute content_hash from prefix_tokens stored in handle and compare against KvPrefixHandle.content_hash — mismatch returns KvCacheError("prefix handle hash mismatch"). evict_all clears all prefix entries + calls llama_kv_cache_clear. set_quantization with KvQuantSupport::None on a model whose capability says supports_kv_quantization=None returns CapabilityNotSupported. Tests (env-gated): load model, prefix_commit some tokens, generate, prefix_restore returns hit, hit counter increments; tamper handle hash, restore fails; switch quant from f16 to q4 — verify context rebuilt and prefix cache flushed.
risk_if_missed: KV caching technique (INF-2 PRODUCTION) has no concrete LlamaCppRuntime impl; spec §4.7.1b technique fails AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.
validator_focus: Quant switch invalidates prefix cache (correctness); tamper detection on restore; LRU eviction does not leak memory under load; capability gate honored.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Quant switch flushes prefix cache (verify hit miss on restore after switch).
LRU eviction bounded (memory does not grow unbounded under 1000 commits).

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: Implemented LlamaCppRuntime KV cache control with declared capability gating, q4/q8 context-param mapping for newly-created native contexts, quant-switch prefix invalidation, bounded prefix snapshot store with TTL/max-bytes LRU behavior, tamper/mismatched-prefix rejection, and env-gated native GGUF proof. This is supporting implementation/test evidence only; WP remains In Progress and Integration Validator PASS remains pending.
implementation_notes: Local llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 exposes LlamaContextParams::with_type_k/with_type_v and context state copy/restore, but does not expose a safe exact live KV occupancy API. occupancy() therefore reports prefix snapshot bytes used and an estimated capacity from model dimensions/type size rather than claiming exact engine-live bytes.

set_quantization is modeled as future-context policy: changing quantization clears stored prefix snapshots and subsequent native contexts use the selected type_k/type_v. It does not mutate an in-flight llama.cpp KV cache in place.

evict_all clears the in-process prefix snapshot store and calls clear_kv_cache on a fresh native context because the current adapter design uses per-generation contexts rather than one persistent live context.
Unsupported declared KV quantization levels are rejected before native context construction; no-quant model specs reject q4/q8 requests.

external_review_supporting_evidence.summary: Subagent reviewed local llama-cpp-2 APIs and identified exact live occupancy as unsupported, plus hardening needs for capability gating, bounded LRU/TTL/max-bytes, quant-switch invalidation, honest stats, and mismatched prompt-prefix validation. The final implementation and tests incorporate those hardening items.

high_roi_additions: Added negative tests for unsupported quantization declarations so future model specs cannot silently enable unavailable KV quantization.
Added quant-switch prefix-flush coverage to prevent stale F16/q4/q8 state restore after policy changes.
Added max-bytes bounded LRU coverage to prevent unbounded prefix snapshot growth during repeated commits.
Added prompt-prefix mismatch generation coverage so restored prefix handles cannot be applied to a different prompt prefix.

proof_commands.note: Initial RED failed because LlamaCppRuntime had no llama_cpp_kv_cache access surface.
5 passed: native quant/prefix happy path, unsupported quantization rejection, quant-switch flush, max-bytes bounded LRU, mismatched prompt-prefix generation failure.
Default disabled-native compile exits 0; remaining warnings are pre-existing crate noise outside MT-075 llama.cpp files.

proof_commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features llama-cpp-runtime-engine --test llama_cpp_kv_cache_tests; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features llama-cpp-runtime-engine --test llama_cpp_generate_tests; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features llama-cpp-runtime-engine --test llama_cpp_loader_tests; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features llama-cpp-runtime-engine --test model_runtime_kv_cache_tests; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features llama-cpp-runtime-engine --test model_runtime_contract_tests
proof_commands.note: kv_cache_exit_code=0, generate_exit_code=0, loader_exit_code=0, model_runtime_kv_cache_exit_code=0, model_runtime_contract_exit_code=0, overall_exit_code=0.
No-default-features check still fails because storage/governance_check_runner modules are feature-gated out while referenced by kernel modules; this is pre-existing/unrelated to MT-075.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-075; this record must not be treated as authoritative WP pass.

The original MT goal mentions bytes_used from llama_cpp_2 kv_cache state; the local safe crate does not expose exact live occupancy, so validation should decide whether prefix-snapshot stats plus estimated capacity satisfy the product intent or require unsafe/sys-level extension later.
Per-generation native context design means evict_all is effective for stored prefix snapshots but does not clear a long-lived live context because such a context is not currently held.
Native llama.cpp checks still require LLVM/libclang, CMake, short CARGO_TARGET_DIR, and HANDSHAKE_TEST_GGUF_PATH setup on this Windows host.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: KV cache + prefix-share + quant-switch + tamper-detect + bounded LRU all implemented and tested, but occupancy() reports estimated capacity rather than live llama.cpp KV bytes due to wrapper API gap; builder discloses this clearly.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: KV cache + prefix-share + quant-switch + tamper-detect + bounded LRU all implemented and tested, but occupancy() reports estimated capacity rather than live llama.cpp KV bytes due to wrapper API gap; builder discloses this clearly.
